name: Hawaii Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Hawaii - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: State Licensing Section - Dietitians
provider.parentOrganization: Hawaii Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Care Assurance

address.description:
DCCA, PVL Licensing Branch
P. O. Box 3469
Honolulu, HI 96801

processingTime: N/A - No state license is required.

requiredDocuments.name: Paper Application
requiredDocuments.description: Obtained by calling the DOH and completed accurately.

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Card
requiredDocuments.description: A copy of your active Commission on Dietetic Registration card.

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of the required accredited degree (foreign degrees require validation).

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Hawaii dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial non-refundable fee for a Hawaii Dietitian License is $225.

mainEntity.name: When does the Hawaii license need to be renewed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Hawaii dietitian licenses are valid for three years from the date of issue and must be renewed triennially. You should contact the DOH to confirm current renewal fees.

mainEntity.name: Can I apply for a Hawaii dietitian license online?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. You must contact the State Licensing Section directly at (808) 692-7400 to request the application packet.

mainEntity.name: Does Hawaii have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Hawaii. To work in Hawaii, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Hawaii?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Hawaii while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
